engineIntelligence.h2: Suzuki Ertiga Engines - Codes, Common Failures & Replacement Costs
engineIntelligence.description: The cards below cover the main engines fitted to the Suzuki Ertiga in the UK, including documented failure causes, variant production years, and typical rebuilt engine costs based on current UK market data.

cards.altText: Suzuki Ertiga D13A engine - diesel 1.3L replacement UK
knownFailures: EGR valve clogging - carbon buildup restricts flow from 60k miles, causing limp mode and poor performance.
Turbo actuator failure - wastegate stickiness leads to boost loss and error codes on high-mileage units.
Injector wear - Bosch piezo injectors degrade after 80k miles, causing rough idle and misfires.

cards.altText: Suzuki Ertiga K14B engine - petrol 1.4L replacement UK
knownFailures: VVT solenoid wear - oil passage clogging causes timing errors and P0011 codes after 70k miles.
Piston ring wear - increased oil consumption reported on high-mileage K14B units over 90k miles.
Ignition coil failure - misfires under load common on city-driven examples with missed services.

cards.altText: Suzuki Ertiga K12M engine - petrol 1.2L replacement UK
knownFailures: SHVS belt tensioner wear - mild-hybrid system belt degradation reported after 60k miles on export units.
Starter-generator faults - 12V-48V conversion component failures on high-mileage SHVS examples.
cards.replacementCost: Typical UK rebuilt engine: £1,350-£2,600 (supply only) Estimated from comparable K12M units - actual quotes may vary.

commonProblems.h2: Suzuki Ertiga Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Suzuki Ertiga's 1.3L D13A diesel and 1.4L K14B petrol engines are generally robust, but EGR clogging, turbo actuator faults and injector wear drive most replacement enquiries for UK models. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty. Use the cost breakdowns below to decide whether repair or replacement offers better value for your Ertiga.

problems.h4: EGR Valve Clogging - Why D13A Diesel Engines Lose Power
problems.affectedModels: Suzuki Ertiga SZ3, SZ5 1.3 DDiS (2015-2018, D13A)
problems.rootCause: Carbon buildup from short-journey driving restricts EGR valve movement, triggering limp mode and poor throttle response.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove, clean or blank EGR valve; reset ECU adaptations
repairOptions.longevity: Temporary fix; may recur if driving pattern unchanged

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Fit new or refurbished EGR valve with updated gaskets
repairOptions.longevity: 2-3 year solution if combined with regular servicing

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with upgraded EGR components, bench-tested
repairOptions.longevity: Long-term solution; 12-month unlimited mileage warranty included

problems.recommendation: If your Ertiga is under 80k miles and otherwise healthy, EGR cleaning or blanking offers good value. For high-mileage units with multiple fault codes, a rebuilt D13A engine often proves more cost-effective than repeated repairs.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt D13A engine prices for your Suzuki Ertiga

problems.h4: Turbo Actuator Fault - Boost Loss on D13A Engines
problems.affectedModels: Suzuki Ertiga SZ3, SZ5 1.3 DDiS (2015-2018, D13A)
problems.rootCause: Wastegate actuator linkage wears or sticks, preventing proper boost control and triggering limp mode.

repairOptions.longevity: Short-term fix; may not resolve worn components

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Fit upgraded actuator per Fiat TSB guidance
repairOptions.longevity: Reliable solution if turbo core is healthy

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Rebuilt engine with upgraded turbo assembly, bench-tested
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for high-mileage engines with multiple issues

problems.recommendation: For isolated actuator faults on otherwise sound engines, specialist actuator replacement is cost-effective. If your D13A has high mileage or additional faults, a rebuilt unit with upgraded turbo components offers better long-term value.
problems.cta: Get quotes for D13A turbo repair or replacement on your Suzuki Ertiga

problems.h4: Diesel Injector Failure - Rough Running on D13A
problems.affectedModels: Suzuki Ertiga SZ3, SZ5 1.3 DDiS (2015-2018, D13A)
problems.rootCause: Bosch piezo injectors wear internally, causing poor atomisation, misfires and increased fuel consumption.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: ECU reset, injector coding, fuel system clean
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable for early-stage issues; not a repair

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Fit flow-tested Bosch injectors with new seals and coding
repairOptions.longevity: Good solution if engine compression is healthy

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Rebuilt engine with calibrated injectors, bench-tested
repairOptions.longevity: Best value when injectors fail alongside other wear items

problems.recommendation: If injector failure is isolated and compression is good, specialist injector replacement is sensible. For high-mileage D13A engines with injector failure plus other symptoms, a rebuilt engine often costs less than cumulative repairs.
problems.cta: Compare D13A injector repair vs rebuilt engine costs for your Suzuki Ertiga

problems.h4: VVT Solenoid Fault - Timing Errors on K14B Petrol
problems.affectedModels: Suzuki Ertiga GL, GLX 1.4 VVT (2015-2018, K14B)
problems.rootCause: Oil sludge clogs VVT solenoid passages, preventing proper cam timing adjustment and triggering P0011 codes.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Clean or replace VVT solenoid; flush oil galleries
repairOptions.longevity: Effective if caught early; requires regular oil changes

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace solenoid, check timing chain tension, update ECU
repairOptions.longevity: Good preventative measure for high-mileage K14B units

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Rebuilt engine with upgraded VVT components, bench-tested
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for K14B with multiple wear-related issues

problems.recommendation: For isolated P0011 codes on otherwise healthy K14B engines, solenoid replacement plus oil system flush is cost-effective. If your petrol Ertiga has high mileage or additional faults, a rebuilt K14B offers better long-term reliability.
problems.cta: Get quotes for K14B VVT repair or rebuilt engine for your Suzuki Ertiga

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Suzuki Ertiga
finalCta.paragraph: The Suzuki Ertiga's compact MPV practicality makes it popular with families and taxi operators, but engine issues can quickly escalate in cost. EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Suzuki Ertiga specialists, with every rebuilt engine including upgraded components addressing known failure points and a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty. Whether you need a quick EGR fix or a complete engine replacement, compare quotes today and get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Suzuki Ertiga Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Suzuki Ertiga and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineYears.h2: Suzuki Ertiga Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the Suzuki Ertiga's UK production timeline (2015-2018), organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt engine prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The Suzuki Ertiga launched in the UK with the Fiat-sourced D13A diesel and K14B petrol engines, establishing the YE0 chassis platform.
keyChanges: First UK registrations of Suzuki Ertiga MPV
D13A diesel and K14B petrol engines introduced
SZ3 and SZ5 trim levels launched
knownFor: Strong initial demand from family buyers
Early D13A units showed EGR sensitivity on short journeys
K14B praised for simplicity in grey import market
years.cta: Get quotes for 2015 Suzuki Ertiga replacement engines
years.ticker: Suzuki Ertiga 2015 engine replacement • D13A EGR fault fix • K14B VVT solenoid replacement

years.description: 2016 saw peak UK Ertiga sales with refined D13A calibration and growing taxi/fleet adoption.
keyChanges: SZ5 SE trim added with enhanced equipment
D13A engine calibration updates for emissions compliance
knownFor: D13A reliability improved with service adherence
Turbo actuator faults emerged on high-mileage taxi units
Strong residual values for well-maintained examples
years.ticker: Suzuki Ertiga 2016 turbo actuator fix • SZ5 diesel engine replacement • Taxi Ertiga engine quote

years.description: Late-production 2017 Ertigas received minor D13A oil system refinements ahead of UK model discontinuation.
keyChanges: Updated D13A oil pump specifications on late builds
knownFor: Late 2017 D13A units showed fewer oil pressure warnings
K14B export models gained VVT solenoid upgrade kits
Growing used-market demand for low-mileage examples

faq.h2: Suzuki Ertiga Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Suzuki Ertiga engine replacement cost UK?
items.answer: Suzuki Ertiga engine replacement typically costs £1,350-£3,200 for a rebuilt unit supply-only, depending on engine code (D13A diesel or K14B petrol), variant and supplier. Supply & fit adds £450-£850.
keyPoints: D13A diesel rebuilt: £1,595-£3,200 supply only
K14B petrol rebuilt: £1,450-£2,800 supply only
All prices include 12-month unlimited mileage warranty
Supply & fit available nationwide via vetted specialists
items.cta: Compare Suzuki Ertiga engine replacement prices now

items.question: What engine codes fit the Suzuki Ertiga?
items.answer: UK Suzuki Ertiga models use the D13A 1.3L diesel (SZ3/SZ5) or K14B 1.4L petrol (GL/GLX grey imports); export models may have K12M 1.2L MHEV.
keyPoints: D13A: 1.3L diesel, 88-92hp, 2015-2018 UK models
K14B: 1.4L petrol, 91-94hp, grey imports and early UK
K12M: 1.2L MHEV, export SHVS variants 2019+
Enter your reg for instant engine code verification
items.cta: Verify your Suzuki Ertiga engine code instantly

items.answer: Yes, professionally reconditioned Suzuki Ertiga engines from vetted UK specialists are reliable, with worn components replaced and full bench testing before dispatch, backed by 12-month warranty.

Bench-tested for 2+ hours before UK delivery
12-month unlimited mileage warranty standard
Choose suppliers with Suzuki Ertiga-specific experience

items.question: Can I use a used Suzuki Ertiga engine?
items.answer: Used Suzuki Ertiga engines are viable for budget replacements on younger vehicles under 70k miles, but verify service history, compression results and warranty terms before purchase.
keyPoints: Used engines: £850-£1,950 supply only
Request compression test and service history
Shorter warranty periods typical vs rebuilt units
Best for low-mileage, well-documented donor vehicles

items.question: Will a Suzuki Ertiga engine pass MoT after replacement?
items.answer: Yes, rebuilt Suzuki Ertiga engines supplied through our network are Euro 5 compliant and include emissions documentation to support MoT testing after installation.
keyPoints: All rebuilt D13A/K14B engines meet Euro 5 standards
Emissions compliance certificates provided
DPF/EGR systems inspected or upgraded as needed
Documentation supports MoT emissions testing

items.question: Do I need ECU recoding for a Suzuki Ertiga engine swap?
items.answer: Most Suzuki Ertiga engine replacements use pre-flashed ECUs compatible with your variant, eliminating dealer recoding; immobiliser sync is handled by our specialists during supply & fit.
keyPoints: Pre-flashed ECUs included with rebuilt engines
Immobiliser sync handled during professional installation
No dealer visit required for most replacements
Confirm ECU compatibility when requesting quotes
items.cta: Compare Suzuki Ertiga ECU-compatible engine options

items.question: What oil should I use after Suzuki Ertiga engine replacement?
items.answer: Use fully synthetic 5W-30 ACEA C3 oil for D13A diesel Suzuki Ertiga engines, or API SN 10W-40 for K14B petrol units; specification sheets are included with every rebuilt engine.
keyPoints: D13A diesel: 5W-30 ACEA C3 fully synthetic
K14B petrol: 10W-40 API SN synthetic recommended
Oil specification sheet included with every engine
Correct oil extends rebuilt engine lifespan
